hints.junior: Font-induced CLS is invisible until you see the filmstrip. Run Lighthouse on Slow 4G — the filmstrip will show the FOUT moment clearly.
hints.mid: font-display: swap is in every Google Fonts embed by default. It prevents invisible text but creates CLS — the metric-matched fallback is the fix.
hints.staff: CrUX source attribution in Chrome 108+ labels layout-shift sources. 'text' source attribution with high CLS on above-fold content = font-induced shift.

hints.junior: The broken version is like most sites using Google Fonts — font-display: swap with no fallback match. The fixed version uses metric overrides to make the swap invisible.
hints.mid: The difference in Lighthouse score between the two versions is typically 0.25+ CLS points — the difference between 'poor' and 'good' in Core Web Vitals.
hints.staff: screenspan.net/fallback is the fastest way to get metric values for Google Fonts. For self-hosted, fontpie CLI does the same calculation.

hints.junior: The concept chapter is the principle — read it before the demo so the toggle makes sense.
hints.mid: Connect the principle to your component API — what prop or CSS change encodes the fix?
hints.staff: Map the pattern to observability: what would you measure in RUM or lab to prove the fix?

hints.junior: Skim the code patterns here — you can copy the structure even if the syntax is new.
hints.mid: Compare broken vs fixed implementation — the diff is usually smaller than you expect.
hints.staff: Check edge cases: SSR, hydration, design tokens, and high-contrast mode.

anecdotes.junior: The first CLS bug I debugged was a hero headline that jumped 40px when the font loaded. Metric-matched fallback fixed it without changing the visual design at all.
anecdotes.mid: font-display: optional is underused — for decorative fonts that aren't critical to the experience, not swapping at all is often the best UX outcome.
anecdotes.staff: size-adjust, ascent-override, and descent-override are CSS spec additions specifically designed for this use case. They landed in all major browsers in 2021-2022.

sections.junior: Hey! Today we're fixing the headline that jumps when the page loads. Fonts are a hidden source of layout shift — and there's a clean CSS fix.
sections.mid: This case covers font-induced CLS — why font-display: swap causes layout shift, and how metric-matched fallback fonts make the swap invisible.
sections.staff: We're covering font-display modes, the metric override properties, tooling for calculating values, and CrUX source attribution for measuring font CLS in production.
